description: Loop reliability improvements: per-task retry tracking with auto-block, model escalation on consecutive failures, environment checks pre-loop, verify command post-iteration, taskType-driven model/timeout selection, progress file naming fix, IterationOutcome::Stale → NoEligibleTasks rename, and skill/prompt updates.

criteria: Rust: No warnings from cargo check or cargo clippy
Rust: All tests pass with cargo test
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
Migration v13 must be backward-compatible: existing databases upgrade cleanly

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Approach before code. Anticipate edge cases. Consider 2-3 approaches, pick the best
2. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable code, wired in according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS — Self-critique after code. Compiles, type-checks, all tests pass
4. CODE QUALITY — Quality dimensions explicit. Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — read qualityDimensions on every task; know what 'good' looks like before coding
Approach before code — consider 2-3 approaches with tradeoffs, pick the best, then implement
Each task delivers working code + tests together
Self-critique after code — review your own implementation for correctness, style, and performance before moving on
Test boundaries and exceptions — edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
.unwrap() in production code paths — use ? with TaskMgrResult

acceptanceCriteria: After non-Completed iteration outcome: UPDATE tasks SET consecutive_failures = consecutive_failures + 1 WHERE id = ?
After Completed outcome (in complete.rs): UPDATE tasks SET consecutive_failures = 0 WHERE id = ?
When consecutive_failures >= max_retries AND max_retries > 0: auto-block task with descriptive last_error
Auto-block sets task status to 'blocked' and last_error to 'Auto-blocked after N consecutive failures'
Auto-block does NOT fire when max_retries = 0 (disabled)
Auto-block does NOT fire on first attempt (consecutive_failures must exceed threshold)
The increment and auto-block check happen in a single DB transaction
All TEST-INIT-003 tests pass
userStories.description:
Implement retry tracking in engine.rs (increment on failure) and complete.rs (reset on success). Add auto-block logic when threshold exceeded.

DO: Increment consecutive_failures after non-Completed outcomes. Reset to 0 in complete.rs. Auto-block when >= max_retries and max_retries > 0.
DO NOT: Implement model escalation — that's FEAT-004. Keep the DB update minimal.

Edge cases:
- max_retries=0 disables auto-block entirely
- Auto-block only after threshold crossed, not on first failure
- Reset only on THAT specific task's completion
edgeCases: max_retries=0 → auto-block disabled
First failure with max_retries=1 → auto-block immediately (threshold is 1)
Complete resets counter for that task only, not others

failureModes.cause: DB update and auto-block check not atomic
Reset happens on any completion, not just the failing task
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Reset is per-task: WHERE id = ? with the specific task_id

userStories.notes: In engine.rs, after processing an iteration outcome, check if it was non-Completed. If so, increment consecutive_failures. Then check if consecutive_failures >= max_retries && max_retries > 0. In complete.rs, after marking a task done, reset consecutive_failures = 0.

correctness: Auto-block must not fire on first attempt — only after max_retries consecutive failures
consecutive_failures counter must reset to 0 on successful completion
performance: No additional DB queries per iteration for retry tracking — piggyback on existing update
style: Follow existing engine.rs pattern for DB updates
No .unwrap() in production code — use ? with TaskMgrResult

userStories.title: Engine retry tracking + auto-block

acceptanceCriteria: After 2 consecutive failures (consecutive_failures >= 2): auto-escalate model via escalate_model()
Escalation integrates with existing check_crash_escalation() function
Escalation from sonnet → opus (not opus → something higher)
If already at opus, escalation is a no-op
Escalation happens BEFORE auto-block check (at 3 failures, escalation at 2 gives one more chance)
userStories.description:
Add model escalation on consecutive failures. After 2 consecutive failures, escalate the model (sonnet → opus) to give the task a better chance before auto-block at 3.

DO: Check consecutive_failures >= 2 and call escalate_model(). Integrate with existing crash escalation pattern.
DO NOT: Create a new escalation mechanism — reuse the existing escalate_model() infrastructure.

Edge cases:
- Already at opus model → escalation is a no-op
- Escalation at 2, auto-block at 3 → gives opus one attempt before blocking
edgeCases: Already at opus model → escalation is no-op
Escalation at 2 failures, auto-block at 3 → one opus attempt before block

failureModes.cause: Escalation fires after auto-block already set
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Escalation must run BEFORE auto-block check in the failure handling path

userStories.notes: The existing check_crash_escalation() pattern in engine.rs is the model to follow. Escalation should be additive — integrate into the same flow, don't create a parallel mechanism.

correctness: Escalation at 2 failures, auto-block at 3 — order matters
Already at opus → no-op, no error
performance: No additional DB queries — escalation is an in-memory model string swap
style: Integrate with existing check_crash_escalation() pattern
No .unwrap() in production code — use ? with TaskMgrResult

userStories.description:
Extract the 65-line retry tracking block from run_loop (lines 1829-1893) into a dedicated handle_task_failure() function. The block chains increment_consecutive_failures → escalate_task_model_if_needed → should_auto_block → auto_block_task with 5 levels of nesting. Extracting it reduces run_loop complexity and improves testability.

DO: Extract into a function with clear signature, preserve transaction atomicity, preserve all warning eprintln! messages.
DO NOT: Change behavior, add new features, or refactor other parts of run_loop.
userStories.exactFix: Extract lines 1829-1893 into pub fn handle_task_failure(conn: &Connection, task_id: &str, outcome: &IterationOutcome) -> TaskMgrResult<()>
userStories.notes: Pure refactoring. The new function should take conn (not tx) and manage its own transaction internally. Outcome filtering (the matches! guard) can stay in run_loop or move into the function.
userStories.rootCause: Retry tracking logic (increment → escalate → auto-block) was added inline in run_loop during FEAT-003/004 implementation, creating a 65-line nested block

userStories.description: Empirical verification of REVIEW-001 showed that the detached cleanup thread dies when the parent task-mgr process exits. For `task-mgr curate dedup`, batches spawned in the final 30s of execution leave orphan jsonl files behind because the 30s timer never fires before the process terminates. Observed: 2 of 5 argv-logged sessions left surviving ai-title files after dedup completed, even 1+ minute later. Fix options (pick one): (a) Track pending cleanup handles and .join() them at the end of curate_dedup/curate_enrich; (b) Replace the 30s delay with a synchronous post-wait cleanup in spawn_claude (1-2s sleep inline after child.wait()); (c) Register an atexit-like mechanism. Option (a) preserves non-blocking semantics while guaranteeing cleanup; option (b) is simplest. Acceptance: rerun the REVIEW-001 empirical test — 0 orphan files remain after dedup returns (or within a short bounded window after).
userStories.title: Cleanup thread dies when task-mgr exits — flush pending cleanups before return
